locked_decisions.D1_env_set: Exactly five ENV params: FILETUBE_YTDLP_ENABLED (off by default, truthy-only), FILETUBE_YTDLP_COOKIES_FILE, FILETUBE_YTDLP_POLL_MINUTES (0 = manual-only, mirrors scanIntervalMinutes), FILETUBE_YTDLP_DOWNLOAD_DIR, FILETUBE_YTDLP_VERSION (build-time pin).
locked_decisions.D2_cookies_membersonly: Cookies file only (no user/pass). Members-only skipped unless the toggle allows AND a cookies file is present. Fail-safe.
locked_decisions.D3_delete_semantics: Delete in FileTube = STAYS GONE. download-archive KEEPS the entry so a deleted video is NOT re-downloaded on next poll (Dean consciously reversed the vision doc's 'delete = re-downloadable'). A 'force re-pull' that bypasses the archive is OUT OF SCOPE for v1.11.0. re-pull-all/re-pull-one = 'check for NEW videos now' (respects the archive), never re-downloads existing/deleted ones.
locked_decisions.D4_ui: DEDICATED PAGE (e.g. /subscriptions) linked from Settings, NOT embedded in Settings. When disabled, the nav link AND the route do not exist (structurally absent, not CSS-hidden).
locked_decisions.D5_ytdlp_update: Pinned in the image (FILETUBE_YTDLP_VERSION) + updated by rebuilding/pulling a new FileTube image. NO runtime auto-update, NO in-app update action for v1.11.0.
locked_decisions.residual: Derive subscription display name from yt-dlp metadata at add-time; simple last-checked + ok/error status string; one global cookies file (not per-sub).

branch: TBD (coordinator-owned git; likely feature/yt-dlp-integration off main)
hard_constraint: OPTIONAL / ADDITIVE / MUST-NOT-DEGRADE. When DISABLED (the default) FileTube behaves BYTE-IDENTICALLY to today: no new routes registered, no background job started, no subscriptions UI, no assumption yt-dlp is installed. The existing 241-test suite stays green with zero behavior change. There MUST be an explicit test/verification that the disabled path is a no-op. This is the acceptance north star.
architecture_decided: Optional, dormant-by-default, IN-PROCESS module inside FileTube (NOT a standalone service — that reintroduces the two-systems/orphan problem). yt-dlp BUNDLED IN THE CONTAINER (reusing the FFmpeg yt-dlp needs) but dormant unless enabled via ENV. Pin a yt-dlp version, keep updating straightforward (PE decides pip vs binary). Subscription config persists in db.json via the v1.9.0 serialized updateDatabase primitive (one source of truth). Downloads land in a media folder the existing scanner indexes -> appear in normal UI -> deleting in FileTube removes them everywhere (no orphans). Dedup via yt-dlp built-in --download-archive. Premiere handling via POLL-AND-DEFER (~2h window), NOT live per-video timers (v1.9.0 lesson). Members-only skip as an ISOLATED, easily-updatable shouldSkip(videoMeta) rules layer, FAIL-SAFE (skip+log on uncertainty). Members-only auth via yt-dlp --cookies file (NOT username/password); without creds members-only is skipped.
security_flags: Module spawns yt-dlp as a CHILD PROCESS with user-provided channel URLs. NEVER shell-interpolate: use execFile/spawn with an ARGUMENT ARRAY (no shell:true). Validate/normalize subscription URLs. Constrain download path so a subscription cannot write outside the media dir (path-traversal guard). Cookies/creds: never log them; treat ENV as a path to a mounted read-only file; no secrets in db.json. High-risk surface -> FULL two-reviewer QA gate (quality-assurance agent + separate /code-review) on command injection, path traversal, creds handling, and the disabled-path-is-a-no-op guarantee.

tasks.round2_result: T4 FIX ROUND #2 (T4b) -- seam hardening + one Dean-decided semantic change. Routed straight-to-SDE (no PE session: D1 is a well-specified tweak the product owner already ruled on and does NOT touch the fresh-install no-op ACs 1-6). D1 CRITICAL prune-on-disable data loss -- extraScanRoots returns [] when DISABLED, so a disabled module's downloadDir leaves the scan set and prune-missing (default ON, server.js:1044 thumbnails / :1053 transcode sidecars / :1116 db.progress) reaps every previously-downloaded id on the next scan; the mount-loss guard (server.js:444) only protects configured-but-unmounted roots, not no-longer-configured ones, so downloaded ids fall through to prune. DEAN'S DECISION: disabling must NOT destroy already-downloaded library content. FIX: extraScanRoots(config) returns [path.resolve(downloadDir)] whenever the dir EXISTS ON DISK (fs.existsSync), [] only when absent -- do NOT gate on config.enabled (parseYtdlpConfig computes downloadDir regardless of enabled). REFRAMED INVARIANT: never-enabled=>dir never created=>[]=>fully inert (optional/additive holds byte-for-byte); enabled=>ensureDownloadDir created it=>scanned; was-enabled-now-disabled=>dir exists with content=>still scanned=>prune-missing never reaps it. Update the code docstring + ARCHITECTURE.md + exec plan disabled-invariant wording to the reframed version. Tests: change ytdlp-scan-root.test.js 'disabled=>not scanned' to 'disabled AND dir absent=>not scanned (inert)' + NEW 'disabled AND dir exists with content=>still scanned (preserved)'; ADD a footgun-closed regression: enable->simulate a downloaded+indexed file (with thumbnail + db.progress)->disable->scan with pruneMissing ON->assert the id + thumbnail + transcode sidecar + db.progress ALL SURVIVE. D2 HYGIENE upgrade-path stale db.folders (dev/test-only -- lib/ytdlp never shipped): the PRE-FIX branch d0f53a0 pushed downloadDir into db.folders; add an idempotent migration (in ensureDownloadDir or startBackground) removing config.downloadDir from db.folders if present (module now owns that root via extraScanRoots); test on an upgraded db.json (pre-seed db.folders with downloadDir -> assert removed + not surfaced by GET /api/config + content still scanned via extraScanRoots); reconcile with D1. D5 survivorIds not de-duped -- yt-dlp can list the same video under multiple tabs; both survive => id twice => 'downloaded 2' + duplicate watch?v=X positional; de-dupe via Set before building download args/count. D6 COSMETIC/DECIDE (OVERRIDES a round-#1 low-tail item): STOP scrubbing availability + REMOVE scrubAvailabilityForLog -- availability is a non-secret yt-dlp enum, scrubbing adds a helper+regex for zero security benefit and hides the skip reason from an operator; log it plainly; SF1 cookies-path redaction is the only redaction that matters and STAYS. D7 optional: (a) all-videos-rejected-as-unsafe-id -> distinct status instead of 'ok: no new videos' -- near-impossible for real ids, SKIP unless trivial; (b) efficiency nits -- pass the already-parsed config into extraScanRoots at server.js:938 to avoid re-parse+path.resolve every scan, and avoid the double fs.existsSync per cycle -- do if cheap else tech-debt-track. tasks.review_result: T4 FIRST TWO-REVIEWER GATE VERDICT: CHANGES REQUESTED. Both reviewers (quality-assurance agent + the separate adversarial /code-review, 11 findings) INDEPENDENTLY CONFIRMED the same architectural critical. What HELD (no churn): loop robustness -- no lock held across the download await, no unhandled rejection, status redaction (SF1) intact, disabled=provable no-op. Routing a T4-FIX round: PE DESIGN for C1 (+reconcile C3/C7 into ONE folder-registration approach) FIRST, then SDE implements the whole set, then build-verify, then re-run the two-reviewer gate. CONFIRMED FINDINGS: C1 CRITICAL filter-bypass (index.js:170) -- runDownload(sub,config) downloads the WHOLE channel URL; isArchived/shouldDeferPremiere/shouldSkip only gate an integer survivorCount whose ONLY effect is whether ANY download runs, never WHICH videos; buildYtdlpDownloadArgs passes only channelUrl+--download-archive (no --match-filter/--playlist-items/per-id list) so --download-archive is the ONLY bound. Two breaches: (a) premiere/live-hang -- a deferred is_live video is un-counted but still fetched by the whole-channel download -> yt-dlp records the livestream until DEFAULT_DOWNLOAD_TIMEOUT_MS(60min) SIGKILL, never archived, recurs every cycle, wedges the sequential poll ~60min/cycle (the exact hang poll-and-defer existed to prevent); (b) members-only bypass (violates D2) -- shouldSkip marks a subscriber_only video skip, but cookiesArgs attaches --cookies whenever the file EXISTS (independent of the toggle) so the single whole-channel download authenticates and fetches the members-only content the operator disabled. PE PICKS the download-scoping mechanism (coordinator recommends option 1: per-survivor https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=<id> targets built from the JS-filtered survivor id set, making skip/defer STRUCTURALLY binding on the child process; ids already constrained by url.js /^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$/ <=64; compose with --download-archive; keep -- separator + host-allowlist discipline; buildYtdlpDownloadArgs signature/shape changes to accept a target list). Option 2 (--match-filter mirroring the JS rules) rejected = two sources of truth; option 3 (--playlist-items) rejected = fragile indices. C2 HIGH correctness (index.js:145 + rules.js:64-68) -- isArchived case-sensitive needle vs yt-dlp's lowercased archive lines; real 'Youtube' TitleCase extractor_key never matches -> every archived video counts as survivor -> redundant work + false 'ok: downloaded N' status; fix: lowercase before compare / prefer video.extractor; MUST fix the unrealistic lowercase 'youtube' test fixtures (test/integration/ytdlp-poll.test.js:95,140) to real 'Youtube' TitleCase and assert corrected behavior. C3 HIGH integration (index.js:484 + server.js:1257) -- POST /api/config rebuilds db.folders=validFolders wholesale (fs.existsSync-filtered); any save omitting downloadDir, or made while the volume is transiently unmounted, evicts it -> downloads never indexed (AC17 broken) until restart; fix: re-register downloadDir at runtime / make scan aware of it independent of db.folders; reconcile with C7. C4 HIGH filter (index.js:140) -- cookiesConfigured=Boolean(config.cookiesFile) is path-set not file-exists, inconsistent with cookiesArgs's fs.existsSync; a set-but-unmounted path -> shouldSkip thinks cookies available -> members-only survives -> download fails -> 'error' status instead of clean skip; fix: ONE shared fs.existsSync helper for 'cookies actually usable'. C5 MEDIUM robustness (index.js:251) -- pollBusy overlap guard drops a coalesced re-pull with no follow-up flag (esp. pollMinutes=0 manual-only, lost forever behind a 202); fix: pending-rerun flag re-running ONCE after the in-flight poll (the v1.9.0 deferred-rescan single unref'd follow-up pattern, no unbounded queue). C6 MEDIUM quarantine (index.js:171) -- runSubscriptionCycle early-returns at !downloadResult.ok BEFORE quarantineEscapedDownloads, yet processSubscription still fires scanDirectories unconditionally -> a path-escaping symlink from a partial-success-then-nonzero-exit download is never unlinked and gets indexed; fix: run confinement quarantine BEFORE the scan on ALL paths (success AND failure), preserving the 'quarantine BEFORE scanDirectories' invariant. C7 MEDIUM lifecycle (index.js:504) -- disabling the module never de-registers downloadDir (startBackground early-returns); core scanner keeps indexing it + GET /api/config keeps listing it -> 'disabled==byte-identical to never-enabled' guarantee broken; PE to reconcile C3+C7 into ONE folder-registration approach (lean toward de-register-on-disabled OR scan downloadDir via a module-owned path rather than injecting into db.folders at all). LOW/tech-debt tail (fix the cheap ones): quarantineEscapedDownloads doesn't recurse into subdirs (yt-dlp -o can nest) -- recurse or assert flat output; an unredacted `availability` in a console.log -- scrub it; doc filter-order (isArchived->skip->premiere) vs code (isArchived->premiere->skip) -- reconcile; no MAX_STATUS_LENGTH bound test -- add one; ensureDownloadDirRegistered compares raw strings not path.resolve-normalized -- normalize. NEW REGRESSION TESTS the fix MUST add: a C1 test asserting skipped/deferred ids are NEVER passed to the download child (assert on the actual target arg set, NOT merely downloadCalls===1); the C2 fixture correction; a members-only-with-cookies-present-but-toggle-off test proving the disabled id never reaches a spawn.
